gene_region.label: BRCA1 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical BRCA1 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: BRCA1 encodes a tumor-suppressor scaffold for homologous recombination, DNA damage signaling, checkpoint control, replication-fork protection, and chromatin-linked repair.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local BRCA1 bundle is DNA-repair and hereditary cancer oriented. Pathogenic BRCA1 alleles are clinically important, but app-level matches remain research/triage context until confirmed by clinical-grade variant interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: BRCA1 truncating, splice, and damaging missense variants can compromise homologous recombination and hereditary breast/ovarian cancer biology.
Founder variants and VUS records require zygosity, transcript, classification, ancestry, and family-history context before interpretation.
condition_research_overview: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: BRCA1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for BRCA1.
Because BRCA1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use BRCA1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability.
Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000012048: BRCA1 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: BRCA1 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BRCA1 homologous-recombination thesis: the individual may carry a signal relevant to DNA double-strand break repair, replication-fork protection, hereditary breast/ovarian cancer review, or HRD pathway biology. Only externally classified pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1 variants should be escalated toward clinical hereditary-cancer risk interpretation.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG is curated as a BRCA1 marker for Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context, Homologous recombination repair deficiency. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context
research_context: Use this marker as a BRCA1 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG with BRCA1 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG or linked BRCA1 locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context, Homologous recombination repair deficiency
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG as a gene-specific marker for Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context, Homologous recombination repair deficiency and homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability.

variant_records.clinical_parameter_summary: This record describes a recurrent model, haplotype, fusion, or loss-of-function class rather than a simple SNP row.
